function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a UTF-8 text file, relative to the repo root. Each line in the result is prefixed with its exact 1-based line number and a tab (cat -n style, e.g. '    12\tsome code'), so you can cite an exact file:line location. The line-number prefix is DISPLAY ONLY — it is never part of the file on disk, so never include it in edit_file's old_string.

path.description: Path relative to the repo root.

function.name: view_media
function.description: Load an image file from the repo into the conversation so you can see it (the media sibling of read_file — read-only, repo-confined, images only). The image arrives as a content part on the next turn. Only useful when the serving model accepts image input; a text-only model will see a placeholder.

path.description: Path relative to the repo root.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create or overwrite a UTF-8 text file, relative to the repo root.

path.description: Path relative to the repo root.

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Replace an exact string in an existing UTF-8 text file. Prefer this over write_file for ANY change to an existing file: it only needs the changed text, not the whole file, so it is far faster and cheaper on large files. 'old_string' must match the file exactly, including whitespace and indentation, and must be unique unless 'replace_all' is true. Use write_file only to create a new file or do a wholesale rewrite.

path.description: Path relative to the repo root.

old_string.description: Exact text to replace (must match the file verbatim).

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of requiring a unique match (default false).

function.name: list_dir
function.description: List the entries of a directory, relative to the repo root.

path.description: Directory relative to the repo root (default: root).

function.name: run_command
function.description: Run a shell command. Each call runs in a FRESH shell with the working directory already at the repo root, so `cd` and environment changes do NOT persist to later calls — never `cd` to reach a path; use repo-relative paths directly.
